title: rubus acupungens
intro: Rubus aculeatus, also known as the prickly bramble or thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus aculeatus include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, tincture, and salve, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
